features.name: gray_code_self_reference
features.description: Code should include a signal assigned to a gray-code style expression referencing its own bits with a shift, creating a combinational cycle. Example: wire [N-1:0] sig = input ^ {sig[N-2:0], 1'b0};

features.name: combinational_cycle_partial_select
features.description: Code should include a signal whose lower bits drive its own higher bits via partial select assignments, creating a combinational cycle.
features.snippet: wire [2:0] SEL; assign SEL[0] = input; assign SEL[1] = SEL[0]; assign SEL[2] = SEL[1];

features.name: right_shift_self_reference
features.description: Code should include a signal assigned to an expression combining input bits with a right-shift of itself, creating a combinational cycle. Example: wire [N-1:0] sig = input ^ (sig >> 1);

features.name: left_shift_self_reference
features.description: Code should include a signal assigned to an expression combining input bits with a left-shift of itself, creating a combinational cycle. Example: wire [N-1:0] sig = input ^ (sig << 1);

features.name: array_self_reference_chain
features.description: Code should include an array element assigned from another array element with cyclic references, creating a combinational cycle through array indexing.
features.snippet: wire [2:0] array_3 [2]; assign array_3[0] = rand_a[2:0] ^ array_3[1] >> 1; assign array_3[1] = array_3[0];

features.name: variable_width_concat_self_reference
features.description: Code should include a signal driving a wider wire via concat that references the signal's own bits, creating a partial select cycle.
features.snippet: wire [1:0] VAR_B; assign VAR_A = {rand_a[0], VAR_B[0]}; assign VAR_B = (VAR_A >> 1) ^ 2'(VAR_B[1]);

features.name: bitwise_xor_chain
features.description: Code should include a signal assigned to bitwise XOR of input with a shifted portion of itself, creating a combinational cycle.

features.name: replicate_with_self_reference
features.description: Code should include a signal using replication operator with self-reference to create a combinational cycle.

features.name: zero_extension_self_reference
features.description: Code should include a signal using zero extension where bits reference the signal itself, creating a combinational cycle.
features.snippet: wire [7:0] ZX; assign ZX[0] = rand_a[3]; assign ZX[3:1] = 3'(ZX[0]); assign ZX[4] = ZX[1];

features.name: variable_shift_with_self_reference
features.description: Code should include a signal using a variable shift amount with self-reference, creating a combinational cycle.

features.name: addition_chain_with_cycle
features.description: Code should include a chain of additions where signals drive each other cyclically, creating a combinational cycle.
features.snippet: wire [7:0] ADD_A, ADD_B, ADD_C; assign ADD_C = rand_b[7:0] + ADD_B; assign ADD_B = (ADD_A << 4) + rand_a[7:0]; assign ADD_A = {ADD_C[7], 7'd0};

features.name: concat_rhs_cycle
features.description: Code should include a wire where the left side of a concat references the signal itself via right-hand side cycle.
features.snippet: wire [1:0] CONCAT_RHS; assign CONCAT_RHS[0] = rand_a[0]; assign CONCAT_RHS[1] = CONCAT_RHS[0];
